reminders.title: Eye break
reminders.body: Look away from the screen and focus on a distant object for 20 seconds. Blink slowly and let your eyes relax.
reminders.safety_note: This is general visual hygiene guidance, not medical advice. Persistent eye symptoms should be assessed by an eye-care professional.

reminders.title: Posture reset
reminders.body: Relax your shoulders. Keep your head level, wrists straight, elbows close, and both feet fully supported.
reminders.safety_note: Do not force a position that causes pain. Adjust workstation equipment where possible.

reminders.title: Move for a minute
reminders.body: Stand up, walk gently, and loosen your hips and back before returning to work.
reminders.safety_note: Move within your safe range and workplace rules.

reminders.title: Neck and shoulder release
reminders.body: Let your arms rest at your sides. Gently roll your shoulders back, then bring your head back to a comfortable neutral position.
reminders.safety_note: Keep the movement gentle. Stop if you feel dizziness, tingling, sharp pain, or worsening symptoms.

reminders.title: Hands and wrists reset
reminders.body: Take your hands off the keyboard or mouse. Open and close your hands slowly, then return with wrists straight and relaxed.
reminders.safety_note: Avoid forceful stretching. Persistent numbness, weakness, or pain should be checked by a qualified clinician.

reminders.title: Voice and breathing reset
reminders.body: Pause between calls when possible. Relax your jaw, lower your shoulders, and take two slow nasal breaths before the next task.
reminders.safety_note: This is a general comfort prompt. Follow your workplace procedures and seek professional care for persistent voice or breathing symptoms.

reminders.title: Control-room posture scan
reminders.body: Before the next monitoring cycle, check that your screens are easy to view, your shoulders are relaxed, and your feet are supported.
reminders.safety_note: Do not interrupt safety-critical monitoring. Use this prompt only when operationally safe.

reminders.title: Parked mobility break
reminders.body: When parked safely, step out if permitted, walk briefly, and move your ankles, hips, and shoulders before driving again.
reminders.safety_note: Never do this while driving. Follow road, site, and employer safety rules.
